# @zakkster/lite-gc-profiler

> Zero-dependency GC and heap profiler. It exists to make the "zero-GC" claim
> falsifiable rather than asserted: it counts garbage-collection events, times the
> pauses, and gates on them.

## Sources (auto-detected)

- node   -> 'gc'   : precise. perf_hooks 'gc' PerformanceEntries -- kind (minor /
                     major / incremental / weakcb) and pause duration, straight from V8.
- Chrome -> 'heap' : heuristic. performance.memory sampling -- allocation rate from
                     positive deltas, collections inferred from heap drops.
- others -> 'none' : no heap API; only the long-frame anomaly heuristic is available.

`profiler.source` reports which one is live.

## Why major GC is the signal

Minor (Scavenge) collections happen from ambient young-generation allocation even in
careful code. A major (Mark-Sweep-Compact) collection means the heap filled enough to
need a full pass -- that is the pressure a zero-GC hot path must avoid. So the default
gate is `{ maxMajor: 0 }`. Empirically, a pooled (zero-allocation) loop produces zero
GC events; an equivalent loop that allocates per iteration produces majors and hundreds
of ms of pause. This profiler is how you prove which one you wrote.

## API

new GcProfiler(capacity = 256, options?)
  options: { heap = true, autoStart = false }
  capacity: size of the pause-duration percentile window (rounded up to a power of two)

  start() / stop()            attach / detach the perf_hooks GC observer (node)
  record(kind, durationMs)    inject an event (tests, or a custom source)
  sampleHeap(now?, usedBytes?)  sample the heap; Chrome auto-reads performance.memory,
                              elsewhere pass e.g. process.memoryUsage().heapUsed. Zero-alloc.
  markFrame(frameMs)          feed a frame time for the long-frame anomaly heuristic
  summary(meta?)              snapshot (see shape below)
  reset() / destroy()
  getters: supported, source, running, gcCount, majorCount, minorCount

Kind constants: GC_MINOR (1), GC_MAJOR (4), GC_INCREMENTAL (8), GC_WEAKCB (16).

## Summary shape

{
  schema: 'lite-gc/1', source: 'gc'|'heap'|'none', supported,
  gc:   { count, totalMs, maxMs, avgMs, p99Ms, minor, major, incremental, weakcb },
  heap: { supported, used, peak, firstSample, samples, allocBytes,
          allocRateBytesPerSec, gcDrops, freedBytes },
  frames: { count, long }
}

## Gate

checkNoGc(summary, rules?) -> { ok, violations, source }
assertNoGc(summary, rules?) -> throws GcBudgetError, or returns the report

rules (all optional; default { maxMajor: 0 }):
  maxMajor, maxMinor, maxPauseMs, maxTotalMs, maxAllocRate (bytes/sec, heap path)

## Node usage (precise)

  import { GcProfiler, assertNoGc } from '@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler';
  const gc = new GcProfiler().start();
  runHotLoop();
  await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));   // GC entries arrive asynchronously
  assertNoGc(gc.summary());                    // throws if any major GC happened
  gc.stop();

GC entries are delivered between frames by the runtime; the per-frame methods
(sampleHeap, markFrame) allocate nothing.

## Install

  npm i @zakkster/lite-gc-profiler

No dependencies. No peer dependencies.
